question_title: Will FIS's market close price on 2026-04-11 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-31?
summary: FIS stock direction over 7 trading days (March 31 to April 10, 2026). FIS is in a severe 42.7% drawdown from its 52-week high, near its 52-week low ($46.13), with strong negative momentum (-4.7% 5-day, -5.9% 10-day). The broader market is also declining (S&P 500 down 9.4% from high). Monte Carlo simulations with zero drift yield ~50% probability of being higher. The conditional base rate for 40%+ drawdowns (63.8% positive over 8 days, n=47) provides a bullish counterweight, but is discounted because the current drawdown coincides with a broad market selloff rather than being idiosyncratic. The Liberation Day anniversary (April 2) adds potential volatility. Blending these signals, I estimate 46% — modestly below 50%, reflecting persistent negative momentum offset partially by the deep drawdown's mean reversion tendency.

factors.description: Strong negative recent momentum: FIS down 4.7% over 5 days, 5.9% over 10 days, 4.3% over 20 days
Broader market decline: S&P 500 down 9.4% from 52w high, down 7% YTD, at 20-day low — bearish macro backdrop
FIS in deep 42.7% drawdown from 52w high; conditional base rate shows 63.8% positive forward 8-day returns when in 40%+ drawdown (n=47)
FIS only 2.9% above 52-week low ($46.13) — proximity to technical floor may provide support or signal further breakdown
Liberation Day anniversary (April 2) and tariff uncertainty create potential for macro volatility during the 7-day window
Monte Carlo simulations with zero drift show ~50% probability; blended scenario model shows ~49.5%
Supreme Court struck down Trump IEEPA tariffs in Feb 2026, but market continues declining — suggests other headwinds at play

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.46 (46% probability FIS closes higher on April 11 than March 31)**

Key reasoning:
- **7 trading days** to resolution (April 11 is a Saturday → uses April 10 close; Good Friday April 3 is a market holiday)
- **Bearish signals dominate**: FIS has strong negative momentum (-4.7% 5-day) and the broader market is in a downturn (S&P 500 down ~9.4% from highs)
- **Bullish counterweight**: Historical conditional returns show 63.8% positive rate when FIS is in a 40%+ drawdown, but discounted because this coincides with a broad market decline rather than an idiosyncratic FIS event
- **Monte Carlo**: Zero-drift simulation gives ~50%; blended scenarios incorporating momentum and mean reversion give ~49.5%
- **Net assessment**: Modestly below 50% reflecting persistent negative momentum, offset by mean reversion potential at the extreme drawdown level
sources_consulted: [Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (FIS) Latest Stock News & Headlines - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/FIS/news/)
[Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (FIS) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/FIS/)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the latest market close price of FIS. If it is higher than the close price on 2026-03-31, the question will resolve to 'Yes'. The close price will be pulled from the Yahoo Finance API.
fine_print: Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
